message: Last week my 8-year-old asked me why we have the flashlights in the closet and I just... froze. I realized I'd never actually *told* her why we keep certain things ready, so to her it probably just looked like random clutter. So I sat down and walked her through it—not scary, just matter-of-fact. 'Sometimes storms knock the power out, so we have these. Here's where the water jugs are. Here's what we do.' And you know what? Instead of being freaked out, she got *into* it. Started suggesting we add a radio, asked if we could practice finding everything in the dark (thrilling for a kid, apparently). Now she feels like she's part of keeping our family ready, not like something scary is looming. Game changer. Turns out kids worry *less* when they understand what's actually happening instead of sensing mysterious prep-vibes from their parents. Have you found that explaining your weather readiness to your kids makes a real difference in how they handle it?
